summary: PR #2634 converted Samus Missile item TU configuration from NonMatching to Matching, reorganized itsamusmissile.c definitions including the item state table and small callback bodies, cleaned control flow in missile turn logic, updated several Samus Missile prototypes, and made small Link Bomb cleanup changes that reuse existing helper calls and boolean negation style.

key_files.role: Marks melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk changes Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c")

key_files.role: Primary matched item TU; reorganizes state table and callback bodies, adjusts includes, cleans control flow, and changes animation return types to bool.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itsamusmissile.c +66/-65 across 10 hunks
raw_diff: adds ItemStateTable it_803F7340 near top of file
raw_diff: moves itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Coll, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Coll, and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion3_Anim later near related motion functions
raw_diff: it_802B64FC replaces several gotos with direct returns and a combined zero-vector condition
raw_diff: itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Anim and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Anim return type changes from s32 to bool

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for Samus Missile functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itsamusmissile.h +5/-5 across 1 hunk
raw_diff: changes several HSD_GObj or struct HSD_GObj prototypes to Item_GObj and animation return types from long to bool

key_files.role: Adjacent item cleanup; simplifies boolean checks and replaces an inline duplicate path with existing helper calls.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itlinkbomb.c +9/-21 across 8 hunks
raw_diff: repeated it_80272C6C(gobj) == 0 checks become !it_80272C6C(gobj)
raw_diff: removes itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim_inline1 and calls it_8029EC34(gobj) instead
raw_diff: itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim_inline2 calls fn_8029E21C(gobj) instead of it_8029D9A4(gobj, 1, 0)

what_changed: Build configuration changed itsamusmissile.c from NonMatching to Matching.
itsamusmissile.c gained an explicit ItemStateTable it_803F7340 with four motion entries.
Small Samus Missile callbacks were relocated near related motion/state functions rather than being clustered before the main creation function.
it_802B64FC control flow was simplified by replacing generated-looking goto/label exits with direct returns and a combined zero-vector check.
Samus Missile animation callbacks were typed as bool instead of s32/long in source and header declarations.
Several Samus Missile function prototypes were narrowed from HSD_GObj or struct HSD_GObj forms to Item_GObj in the header.
itlinkbomb.c replaced repeated explicit zero comparisons with logical negation and removed one inline helper in favor of an existing function call.

smart_moves.text: Linked the TU by changing only the configure.py object status for itsamusmissile.c, keeping the build-system edit narrow.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py one-line NonMatching to Matching change

smart_moves.text: Made header prototypes more truthful where the item-specific type and bool return were evident from the recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsamusmissile.h prototype changes for it_802B66A8, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_802B6A60, and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Anim

smart_moves.text: Reduced generated-looking branch exits in it_802B64FC without introducing new matching-only data or pragmas.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802B64FC goto block_18 exits replaced by return

smart_moves.text: In itlinkbomb.c, reused existing helper it_8029EC34 instead of keeping a separate inline duplicate body for the UnkMotion3 transition path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim_inline1 and replacement call with it_8029EC34(gobj)

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body proves item-specific callback signatures, update the owning header to use Item_GObj and bool rather than leaving broader HSD_GObj or integer return types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsamusmissile.h changes long animation prototypes to bool
raw_diff: itsamusmissile.h changes several prototypes from HSD_GObj or struct HSD_GObj to Item_GObj

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking goto exit labels can sometimes be collapsed into direct returns while preserving the same local structure for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802B64FC removes block_18 return label and replaces branches with direct return statements

decomp_lessons.lesson: Before retaining a small inline duplicate in item code, check whether an existing local helper already expresses the same transition sequence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itlinkbomb.c removes itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim_inline1 and calls it_8029EC34(gobj)

naming_conventions.observation: The state table kept an address-style symbol name rather than inventing a semantic name.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds ItemStateTable it_803F7340[]

naming_conventions.observation: Local item and attribute variables use short role names already common in decomp item code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Item* ip and itSamusMissileAttributes* attrs in it_802B63F8; itSamusMissile_ClampTurn(Item* ip, itSamusMissileAttributes* sa)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Consistent with canonical control-flow cleanup; matching effect is implied by PR scope but not separately validated in evidence.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c after PR 2634 for prototype or state table cleanup.
Search for other item TUs where animation callbacks changed from s32 or long to bool during Matching linkage.
Search for PRs replacing inline item transition helpers with existing helper calls like it_8029EC34.
Search for remaining PAD_STACK uses in Samus Missile and Link Bomb item functions to see whether they were later removed or justified.
Search for item TUs changed from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) with only narrow configure.py edits.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2634 linked itsamusmissile.c by combining a narrow configure.py Matching flip with source/header cleanup.
accepted_candidate_records.text: For Samus Missile, the PR changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c") to Object(Matching, ...), reorganized state-table and callback placement in itsamusmissile.c, and updated header prototypes to bool/Item_GObj forms where shown in the diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py Matching change
raw_diff: itsamusmissile.c ItemStateTable and callback movement
raw_diff: itsamusmissile.h prototype changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2634 demonstrates small generated-control-flow cleanup in an item update function.
accepted_candidate_records.text: In it_802B64FC, terminal goto/label exits were replaced with direct returns and a combined zero-vector condition, aligning with canonical control-flow cleanup while staying within the item TU match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802B64FC direct return changes

source_update_candidates.title: Header prototype cleanup when item callback bodies prove narrower types
source_update_candidates.text: Consider a standard note that item matching PRs should update owning headers from broad HSD_GObj/integer callback declarations to recovered Item_GObj/bool signatures when the body and call sites support it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsamusmissile.h prototype changes
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR is consistent with the existing truthful-headers standard, but one PR without review comments is not enough to create a new accepted global rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all item state tables should be moved near the top of the file; this PR only shows that placement for it_803F7340 in itsamusmissile.c.
Do not treat PAD_STACK retention in the touched files as endorsed style; the slice contains no verifier justification for those uses.
Do not claim review approval rationale or CI/objdiff validation; no review comments, reviews, or verification logs are loaded.
Do not promote the possible it_802B63F8 source/header type mismatch as a fact without checking current source or full historical context.
